reasoning: Paul Biedermann's 200m freestyle world record of 1:42.00 was set in 2009 during the 'supersuit' era. It has stood for roughly 16 years. This period corresponds to the international short course (25m pool) season, meaning there will be exceptionally few, if any, elite long course events where a world record attempt could realistically occur. Given these factors, it is extremely unlikely that the record will be broken in the next three months. To reflect the requested increasing uncertainty over time while staying within the maximum allowed confidence bounds, probabilities are scaled slightly down for the further resolution dates, but all remain very high reflecting the immense stability of this 16-year-old supersuit record.
